title: Job History Logging in Hadoop MapReduce
description: The 'jobhistory' directory in Hadoop MapReduce is responsible for logging and tracking job events. It comprises multiple classes, each representing different job-related events, implementing the 'HistoryEvent' interface. These classes convert job event data into 'TimelineEvent' objects for YARN's timeline service, ensuring consistent and extensible job history management. The directory focuses on job initialization, priority changes, queue changes, status updates, task completion, and task initiation. It demonstrates a collaborative role across Hadoop and YARN components, crucial for system transparency and debugging.
summary:
TITLE: Job History Logging in Hadoop MapReduce
DESCRIPTION: The 'jobhistory' directory in Hadoop MapReduce is responsible for logging and tracking job events. It comprises multiple classes, each representing different job-related events, implementing the 'HistoryEvent' interface. These classes convert job event data into 'TimelineEvent' objects for YARN's timeline service, ensuring consistent and extensible job history management. The directory focuses on job initialization, priority changes, queue changes, status updates, task completion, and task initiation. It demonstrates a collaborative role across Hadoop and YARN components, crucial for system transparency and debugging.

intermediate_summaries.name: jobhistory chunk 0
intermediate_summaries.summary: The `JobInfoChangeEvent` class, part of Hadoop's job history logging system, represents changes in a job's submit and launch times. It implements the `HistoryEvent` interface, storing job ID, submit time, and launch time in a `JobInfoChange` datum. The core logic revolves around its constructor and `toTimelineEvent()` method, which converts the event into a `TimelineEvent` for YARN's timeline service. This class depends on Hadoop's `JobID`, `StringUtils`, and YARN's `TimelineEvent` and `TimelineMetric` for timeline service integration, alongside Avro's `Utf8` for string handling and Hadoop's classification annotations. It ensures that changes in job submit and launch times are accurately logged and accessible for monitoring and debugging purposes.

intermediate_summaries.name: jobhistory chunk 1
intermediate_summaries.summary: The `jobhistory` node, as part of a larger software architecture, focuses on logging and tracking job events in Apache Hadoop MapReduce. It encompasses several classes, each representing different job-related events such as job initialization, priority changes, queue changes, status updates, unsuccessful completion, and task attempts. These classes implement the `HistoryEvent` interface, ensuring a uniform way to encapsulate job event data. They convert this data into `TimelineEvent` objects for YARN's timeline service, facilitating monitoring and logging. The classes depend on Hadoop and YARN APIs for job identifiers, task types, counters, and timeline service representations, demonstrating a clear shared responsibility and collaboration across components. This hierarchical structure ensures a consistent and extensible approach to job history management, crucial for system transparency and debugging.

intermediate_summaries.name: jobhistory chunk 2
intermediate_summaries.summary: The `jobhistory` node, specifically chunk index 2, focuses on the representation and management of events related to tasks in Apache Hadoop MapReduce. The `TaskFinishedEvent.java` class encapsulates the successful completion of tasks, implementing the `HistoryEvent` interface and converting task completion details into Avro and YARN timeline service formats. The `TaskStartedEvent.java` class represents the initiation of tasks, storing task-specific information in an Avro datum, and converting it into a `TimelineEvent` for YARN. The `TaskUpdatedEvent.java` class handles updates to tasks, preparing a `TimelineEvent` object for the YARN timeline service. All three classes share dependencies on Hadoop and YARN APIs, demonstrating a collaborative role in managing task events across the Hadoop ecosystem. This shared responsibility ensures seamless tracking and reporting of task progress and completion, forming a crucial part of the Hadoop job history architecture.

raw_response:
TITLE: Job History Logging in Hadoop MapReduce

DESCRIPTION:
The 'jobhistory' directory in Hadoop MapReduce is responsible for logging and tracking job events. It comprises multiple classes, each representing different job-related events, implementing the 'HistoryEvent' interface. These classes convert job event data into 'TimelineEvent' objects for YARN's timeline service, ensuring consistent and extensible job history management. The directory focuses on job initialization, priority changes, queue changes, status updates, task completion, and task initiation. It demonstrates a collaborative role across Hadoop and YARN components, crucial for system transparency and debugging.
